title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
summary: Google Cloud and NVIDIA demonstrated GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and Agent Platform on tabular molecular data. The approach achieves 20x-45x training speedups using cuDF and cuML while maintaining compatibility with pandas and scikit-learn APIs.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech walks through a joint Google Cloud and NVIDIA demonstration of GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening for molecular binding prediction at scale. The setup pairs cuDF and cuML to reach 20x-45x training speedups on tabular data without changes to existing pandas or scikit-learn code, then applies the workflow to billion-molecule screening against lung cancer targets such as EGFR. The takeaway: drop-in GPU acceleration of standard tabular ML libraries can compress training time enough to make screening at that volume practical.
